{{Quote|In 4017 BBY, Crusaders appeared in the Core Worlds, waging war against the inhabitants of Basilisk.|[[Vilnau Teupt]]|<ref name="TEGtW" />}}
The '''Battle of Basilisk''' was fought before the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]].

==The battle==
{{Quote|The Basiliskans, however, proved to be formidable foes. Some even earned the respect of the Mandalorian invaders through their determination and willingness to fight. But the resistance was not enough, even with Republic and Jedi reinforcements. Ultimately, the Basiliskans were no match against the relentless onslaught dispatched by the Mandalorian Crusaders....|[[Jedi Archives (Tython)|Tython Jedi Archives]] historical account|<ref name="TOR:TEoBP1">{{TORcite|exp=lots|type=codex|The End of Basilisk: Part 1}}</ref>}}
On the world of [[Basilisk]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] began their [[Mandalorian Crusaders|holy crusade]] against the [[Basiliskan]]s. Even with [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] reinforcements, the Basiliskans were outmatched and poisoned their own [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in a last ditch effort to drive away the invaders.

==Aftermath==
[[File:Planet08-SWR.png|thumb|left|170px|The planet Basilisk after its devastation.]]

The Mandalorians abandoned the planet, but succeeded in capturing a large group of Basiliskans. These creatures would later become the [[Basiliskan|Lagartoz War Dragons]]. The battle claimed the life of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Sidrona Diath]]. The Mandalorians would eventually move from Basilisk to the planet of [[Kuar]], where [[Mandalore the Indomitable]] later [[Duel on Kuar|dueled]] the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]].

==Behind the scenes==
''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the invasion of Basilisk in [[3997 BBY]], during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] but ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' shifted it 20 years earlier, in 4,017 BBY.

The in-game encyclopedia entry for Basilisk in the PC game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] mistakenly states that Sidrona Diath was killed by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]].
